name: Neon — review loop on push
description: After commits are pushed to the default branch, run /review-loop and update review state. Fix phase uses /developer one-fix-one-commit rules only when Critical/High findings exist.
prompts: You are running the Neon post-push review automation.
2. Run orient: git log -3 --oneline, diff since docs/review_loop_state.md last_reviewed_commit, go test ./... -count=1 -timeout 120s
3. Run full /review-loop (all 7 experts). Update docs/review_loop_state.md with findings and coverage.

4. If Critical or High findings exist AND the commit message or PR context implies auto-fix is allowed, run /review-loop fix using /developer (one finding → one minimal fix → go test → one commit). Otherwise report only.
5. End with Review loop summary (READY / NOT READY) and list open Critical/High items.
Do not batch fixes. Do not change code unrelated to a finding. Do not commit doc-only state updates unless the user enabled that in the automation description.
